events.description: Find your "inner magic" during this upbeat, hilarious, and interactive magical experience with BenTastic! Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as he leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "real magicians" during this fast-paced, fun and amazing magic show! Young children must attend with a parent/caregiver. Registration required.  Please arrive on time to secure your spot.  Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form

Space is limited. Large groups must call ahead to attend.

events.title: "Jane and the Blue Willow Princess" with Author Catherine Little
events.description: On a beautiful spring day, sisters Jane and Cassandra enjoy tea with Mother in the garden. Jane is in the throes of writing a story for Father’s birthday, but too many ideas swirl in her head, and she cannot get started. The Blue Willow pattern on the tea set catches Jane’s attention … will that be the inspiration she’s looking for?​ Catherine Little presents a reading from her picture book " Jane and the Blue Willow Princess " about author Jane Austen, then leads a fun craft with air-dry clay.&

events.title: Artists in the Library: Animate with 16mm film

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Soundscape
events.description: Next we will be inspired by the library’s architecture and happenings as we are guided by musician Aniqa Qadir to create a soundscape. Working in groups, visitors will be prompted to absorb the sounds of the building and led through the process of creating an audio journey. This playful session welcomes participants to break the whisper norm of a library and interrupt the space with their observations. Led by Aniqa Qadir and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Raíces — Classical & Latin American Guitar
events.description: Toronto-based guitarist Karla García invites audiences on a musical journey from the elegance of the Baroque era to the rich musical traditions of Latin America. Featuring works by Bach, Dilermando Reis, Víctor Jara, Luiz Bonfá, and Chilean composers Antonio Restucci, alongside original compositions from her upcoming debut album, this concert celebrates the timeless voice of the classical guitar—intimate, soulful, and deeply human.

events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have questions about your tablet, phone, laptop, email or an app? Do you need help with eBooks or other online library services? Bring in your own device and our teen volunteers are here to help!

events.title: Knitting Circle
events.description: Join Port Union Library's Knitting Circle. Chat and knit or crochet with a friendly group! You can also share your projects with others!  This is a drop in program but limited to a room capacity of 20 participants. Arrive early to secure your spot.  No registration required.

events.title: Sunday Family Game Time
events.description: Join us for a family games afternoon from Lego to Uno. We will also have some colouring and crafts for everyone Every Sunday afternoon all ages welcome

events.title: Sunday Family Club
events.description: Enjoy and have fun on Sunday afternoons at Guildwood Library! Will you colour your favourite characters, do a craft or show off your building skills with LEGO and KEVA? Maybe it will be Snap Circuits or Board Games? Every Sunday come in for a surprise!

events.title: Sunday International Chess Club
events.description: Join the Sunday Chess Club, as we play chess every other Sunday. Players of all strengths and skill level are welcomed to participate in the club. Chess introduction lessons are available for players that are new to the game. Sets are provided. The program is open to all ages 7 years and above.

events.title: Youth Hub:  Nail Night
events.description: Unleash your creativity as you design your own stunning nail art! Join us for one or all of our fabulous Nail Nights on the first Monday of every month. Drop-in, no registration required. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Board Game Café
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Tuesday to play board games with your friends! Ask us for our list of board games, and check our monthly calendar for Tournaments with prizes! All materials provided.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.description: Looking for a fun after-school activity? Join our Youth Chess Program to learn new skills, play matches, and connect with others!  This program is open to youth ages 7-17 years old. Facilitated by the University of Toronto Scarborough Chess Club. No registration required. Space is limited.
